lede: A look at January 11 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Möngke Khan and Parmigianino, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Bulo Marer hostage rescue attempt
events.desc: One French soldier and 17 militants are killed in a failed attempt to free a French hostage in Bulo Marer, Somalia.

events.title: Sir Leo Hielscher Bridges
events.desc: The Gateway Bridge, Brisbane, Queensland, Australia is officially opened.

events.title: Louis B. Mayer
events.desc: Louis B. Mayer, head of film studio Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer (MGM), announces the creation of the Academy of Motion Picture Arts and Sciences, at a banquet in Los Angeles, California.

events.title: Occupation of the Ruhr
events.desc: Occupation of the Ruhr: Troops from France and Belgium occupy the Ruhr area to force Germany to make its World War I reparation payments.

events.title: Karluk (ship)
events.desc: The Karluk, flagship of the Canadian Arctic Expedition, sinks after being crushed by ice.

events.title: Lawrence, Massachusetts
events.desc: Immigrant textile workers in Lawrence, Massachusetts, go on strike when wages are reduced in response to a mandated shortening of the work week.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Alabama secedes from the United States.

events.title: William Herschel
events.desc: William Herschel discovers Titania and Oberon, two moons of Uranus.

events.title: Arauco War
events.desc: Arauco War: A Spanish army is defeated by local Mapuche-Huilliches as it tries to cross Bueno River in Southern Chile.

events.title: Theodora Porphyrogenita
events.desc: Theodora is crowned empress of the Byzantine Empire.

births.name: Matteo Renzi
births.line: Italian politician, 56th Prime Minister of Italy

deaths.text: Edmund Hillary, New Zealand mountaineer and explorer
Fabrizio De André, Italian singer-songwriter and guitarist

observances.text: Christian feast day: Anastasius of Suppentonia (Roman Catholic)
Christian feast day: Leucius of Brindisi (Roman Catholic)
Christian feast day: Paulinus II of Aquileia
Christian feast day: Vitalis of Gaza (Roman Catholic)
